summary: Exhaustive open-ended security review of WooCommerce attack surface (1763 files / 89 batches) with Opus 4.8, adversarial verify, then aggregate

all_with_verdicts.title: Coupon description (post_excerpt) echoed without escaping into textarea on coupon edit screen
all_with_verdicts.data_flow: $_POST['excerpt'] saved as shop_coupon post_excerpt -> stored -> edit_form_after_title() echoes $post->post_excerpt raw (no esc_textarea) inside <textarea id="woocommerce-coupon-description"> on the coupon edit screen
all_with_verdicts.entry_point: admin 'edit_form_after_title' action on shop_coupon edit screen (post.php). Authoring a coupon requires publish_shop_coupons (shop_manager/admin). Output is unescaped (phpcs EscapeOutput.OutputNotEscaped explicitly disabled).

verdict.auth_required: administrator (single-site, requires unfiltered_html); not exploitable by shop_manager as claimed
verdict.reasoning:
The sink is real: edit_form_after_title() at class-wc-admin-post-types.php:720 echoes $post->post_excerpt raw (no esc_textarea) inside a <textarea>, with phpcs EscapeOutput explicitly disabled. However, the stored-XSS exploit path does NOT hold at the claimed shop_manager privilege because WordPress core neutralizes the stored value on every write path.

Storage path analysis:
1. On the post.php coupon edit screen, the description field is name="excerpt". It is saved by WordPress CORE edit_post()/wp_update_post(), NOT by WooCommerce. WooCommerce's own coupon save handler (class-wc-meta-box-coupon-data.php:357 save()) never reads $_POST['excerpt'] and never calls set_description() — it only sets code, amounts, dates, etc. So post_excerpt is written through core sanitize_post('db'), whose 'pre_post_excerpt' filter is wp_filter_post_kses, registered by kses_init_filters() for any user WITHOUT the unfiltered_html capability.
2. shop_manager does NOT have unfiltered_html (confirmed in class-wc-install.php:2384-2429 role definition and get_core_capabilities; only WP-core caps + WC shop caps are granted). Therefore kses runs on a shop_manager's input. kses strips <script> (not in $allowedposttags) AND strips the </textarea> closing tag (textarea is not an allowed post tag), so the payload cannot break out of the textarea — it stays inert text inside the field. No XSS.
3. The WC coupon data store update() (class-wc-coupon-data-store-cpt.php:178-208) does use a direct $wpdb->update that bypasses kses, but ONLY when doing_action('save_post'); and in that branch it writes get_description('edit'), which is the value loaded by read() from the DB AFTER core's kses-filtered edit_post() write in the same request. set_description() is never called during the admin save, so the raw $_POST never reaches that $wpdb path. The non-save_post (pure CRUD/REST) branch uses wp_update_post(), which again applies kses for users lacking unfiltered_html. The REST coupon endpoint also requires edit_shop_coupons (shop_manager/admin) and likewise gets kses-filtered.

The only actor who can store raw </textarea><script> is one possessing unfiltered_html — i.e., an administrator on single-site (multisite admins also lack it by default). An administrator already has full unrestricted HTML/JS/theme-edit capability by design, so this is not a privilege-boundary crossing and not a meaningful stored-XSS finding. Conclusion: the missing esc_textarea is a defense-in-depth/hardening nit, but core kses prevents exploitation at the stated shop_manager level. Refuted.
